summary: Iteration 4 (refactor + 4 new features) PASSED 100%. Spectrogram visualizer (before/after STFT canvas), Batch processing (multi-file queue, sequential run, per-track + downloadall), A/B blind test modal (random side, score, reset/next), and Codec round-trip MP3 (auto-on with stealth) all work end-to-end on Chromium with ZERO console errors and ZERO /api/* calls.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js is now a clean thin router (207 LOC) — files state array drives single vs batch (length===1 → SingleTrackView, >1 → BatchView). Good separation of concerns.

BatchView internal `tracks` state is decoupled from App.js `files` prop. When user removes a track via batch-remove, BatchView's tracks array shrinks but parent `files` is NOT updated, so UI stays in batch view even when only 1 track remains. This is consistent with review_request expectation ('UI may stay in batch view') but is a minor UX inconsistency: removing the second-to-last track should probably bubble up to App.js and switch to SingleTrackView. Not blocking.
Codec round-trip succeeded on full stealth pipeline (4s stereo 440Hz WAV) — no 'Codec round-trip skipped' warning was triggered, processed audio src=blob: works, download enabled.

Spectrogram canvas heights measured at 184px (within expected 140-200px). Both spectrogram-original and spectrogram-processed render correctly; processing completes in <60s for stealth preset on 3s stereo input.

ABBlindTest random layout uses Math.random() — A/B sides are randomised per round via useMemo deps [round, originalUrl, processedUrl]. Score increments correctly on guess, persists across next/round, resets to 0/0 on ab-reset. Verdict label only shown after total >= 3 rounds (correctly hidden in our 2-round test).
BatchView sequential processing: 2x 2s mono WAVs → both done with light preset within ~10-15s. batch-downloadall enabled, per-track batch-download-{id} buttons present.
Hero copy correctly mentions 'spectrogram visualizer · A/B blind test · batch processing · codec round-trip'. 'Mode batch' badge with Layers icon present in feature row.

data-testid coverage is complete for all new features: spectrogram-original, spectrogram-processed, ab-button, ab-blind-test-dialog, ab-card-A/B, ab-audio-A/B, ab-pick-A/B, ab-score, ab-next, ab-reset, ab-verdict, batch-section, batch-mode-badge, batch-list, batch-track-{id}, batch-run-button, batch-cancel-button, batch-downloadall-button, batch-download-{id}, batch-remove-{id}, toggle-codecroundtrip.

All previous testids (preset-*, toggle-*, slider-*, format-*, render-options, dropzone, file-input, audio-section, process-button, download-button, reset-button, toggle-random-seed) still functional after refactor.
Files remain modular: SingleTrackView 190 LOC, BatchView 226 LOC, ABBlindTest 149 LOC, SettingsPanel 363 LOC — all under 700 LOC threshold.
seed_data_creation: Generated /tmp/s1.wav (3s 440Hz STEREO 44.1kHz 16-bit, 529KB) for single-track flow. Generated /tmp/a.wav (2s 440Hz mono, 176KB) and /tmp/b.wav (2s 880Hz mono, 176KB) for batch flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure static SPA (no backend). Iteration 4 = major refactor: App.js thin router + 4 new features. All testids verified working. Stealth pipeline now includes codec round-trip (re-encode MP3 320kbps → decodeAudioData) — passed on Chromium without fallback. Spectrogram canvas is ~184px tall, uses STFT FFT 1024/hop 512/0-12kHz custom magma-cyber colormap. A/B test verdict requires score.total >= 3 rounds before showing verdict label. BatchView's internal tracks state is independent of App.js files — removing tracks does NOT auto-switch to single view. Use timeout >= 60s for full stealth processing of 3s stereo. Batch with 2x 2s mono + light preset completes in ~10-15s.

verification_details.title: unmark.audio · AI watermark remover
verification_details.stealth_e2e_with_codec_roundtrip: PASS — processed audio blob: src, download enabled, no 'Codec round-trip skipped' warning
verification_details.batch_remove_track_works: track removed; UI stays in batch view (App.js files unchanged)
